page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.metal-12xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor. This x86_64 architecture configuration features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and does not include any GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the top tier (top 10%) across multiple categories. The dedicated AMD EPYC processor achieves top-tier results in single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached read-only speeds reaching 11403.81 GB/sec and uncached write speeds at 200.67 GB/sec. The server also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in software compilation, cryptographic operations, and LLM inference, processing up to 7012.51 tokens per second for small models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture delivers high resource density and eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly efficient for compute-heavy workloads despite the lack of local NVMe storage or dedicated GPUs. The 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth ensures rapid data transfer for EBS-optimized storage configurations. This server is highly suited for high-performance general-purpose applications, database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.metal-12xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring a dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for EBS-optimized storage. Benchmarks place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. Qualitatively, the bare-metal design offers high resource density and eliminates virtualization overhead, making it highly efficient for demanding workloads. It is optimized for database hosting, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

tagline: A high-frequency bare-metal server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for demanding compute and database workloads.

192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
